summary: Israel announced the deepest IDF operation inside Lebanon, reaching about 12 km, and Hezbollah junior officers have fired a TBM toward Israel without senior approval. Former Israeli PM Bennett advocated legal settlements in West Bank Area C while opposing illegal construction in Area B. Iran’s deputy foreign minister reported a U.S. license for Iranian oil sales and denied any agreement on nuclear inspectors.
key_points: Israel’s IDF conducted an operation 12 km inside Lebanon, marking its deepest incursion to date (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Hezbollah junior officer fired a TBM at Israel without higher‑level authorization, prompting a potential Israeli response (Source: @inon_yttach/9117).
Former PM Bennett called for removal of illegal West Bank construction and supported legal settlements in Area C (Source: @inon_yttach/9128).
Israel’s security chief warned of a possible ground attack on Eilat from Jordan or the sea (Source: @amitsegal/62443).
IDF announced a military exercise in the Gulf of Eilat with active movement of forces but no security incident expected (Source: @pkpoi/142929).
Iran’s deputy foreign minister said the U.S. issued a general license for Iranian oil and petrochemical sales (Source: @presstv/195206).
Iran denied any talks on nuclear inspectors in Switzerland, stating no such agenda was discussed (Source: @VahidOnline/76597).

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s IDF reached 12 km inside Lebanon, the deepest penetration reported, while Hezbollah junior officers fired a TBM at Israel without senior clearance. Former Israeli PM Bennett pushed for legal settlements in West Bank Area C and condemned illegal construction in Area B. Iran announced a U.S. license for oil sales and denied any agreement on nuclear inspections in Swiss talks.

points: Iran’s deputy foreign minister Gharibabadi announced that the United States issued a general license permitting the sale of Iranian oil and petrochemical products (Source: @presstv/195206).
Iranian officials denied that any discussion about International Atomic Energy Agency inspectors took place during Swiss negotiations, calling such reports false (Source: @VahidOnline/76597).
Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson stated that nuclear and missile capabilities were not on the negotiation agenda in Switzerland (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4216).
Iran reported that sanctions on its oil and petrochemical exports were lifted and some frozen assets were released (Source: @VahidOnline/76588).
The IDF reported its deepest operation inside Lebanon, reaching approximately 12 km from the border (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).

Israel’s security chief Zini warned in closed meetings that Eilat could be vulnerable to a ground incursion from Jordan or the sea and ordered intelligence units to prioritize this scenario (Source: @amitsegal/62443).
The Israeli Defense Forces announced a military exercise in the Gulf of Eilat, with heightened movement of security forces and vessels, but no security incident anticipated (Source: @pkpoi/142929).
Israel’s northern reserve units were released from alert status following a ceasefire announcement (Source: @New_security8200/202054).
Israeli officials reiterated that regime troops will continue to occupy southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/195225).

Former Prime Minister Bennett said illegal construction in the West Bank’s Area B should be removed and supported legal settlements in Area C, viewing Area C as future Israeli territory (Source: @inon_yttach/9128).
Hezbollah junior officer fired a TBM toward Israel without senior command approval, raising concerns of escalation (Source: @inon_yttach/9117).
Israel’s IDF operation penetrated 12 km inside Lebanese territory, marking the deepest incursion reported (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).

key_developments: IDF’s 12 km deep operation inside Lebanon signals a significant escalation on the northern front (Source: @idf_telegram/24418).
Hezbollah junior officer’s unauthorized TBM launch toward Israel could trigger retaliatory strikes (Source: @inon_yttach/9117).
Iran’s deputy foreign minister confirmed a U.S. license for Iranian oil sales, affecting regional energy markets (Source: @presstv/195206).
Iran denied any agreement on nuclear inspectors in Swiss talks, maintaining its stance on sovereignty (Source: @VahidOnline/76597).
Former PM Bennett’s call for legal settlements in West Bank Area C may influence settlement policy (Source: @inon_yttach/9128).
Israel’s security chief highlighted Eilat’s vulnerability to a ground attack from Jordan or sea routes (Source: @amitsegal/62443).
IDF announced a Gulf of Eilat exercise with active force movements but no immediate threat (Source: @pkpoi/142929).
sections.threat_assessment: The deep IDF incursion into Lebanon and the unauthorized Hezbollah TBM launch indicate a rising risk of direct confrontation on Israel's northern border. Iran's partial easing of oil sanctions may bolster its regional influence, while its denial of nuclear inspector talks suggests continued diplomatic friction with the United States. Settlement policy shifts in the West Bank could exacerbate Israeli‑Palestinian tensions, potentially destabilizing the broader area.
sections.regional_response: Israel is likely to increase defensive postures in the north, especially around Eilat, and may conduct further operations to deter Hezbollah activity. Iran may leverage the new oil license to fund proxy groups, maintaining pressure on its adversaries. International actors, including the U.S., will monitor Iran’s nuclear stance closely, while European mediators may face challenges addressing settlement expansions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah command communications is essential to detect any escalation following the TBM incident. Satellite and SIGINT assets should track IDF movements near the Lebanese border for signs of further deep incursions. Economic intelligence on Iranian oil exports will help assess funding streams to regional militias. Diplomatic channels must verify Iran’s position on nuclear inspections to anticipate future negotiation dynamics.
